rationale: Neither the original code nor the proposed Lua script contains a remove (SREM) operation, meaning both effectively cap total distinct users over the TTL window rather than currently active concurrent users.
Because SET_KEY is assigned its expiration before TTL_KEY is set, TTL_KEY will expire slightly later, and any SADD occurring in that short window will falsely skip the expiration block.

The Lua script explicitly returns 1 to allow existing members before checking the size limit, whereas the original code evaluated size and rejected requests even for returning users, representing an undocumented behavioral shift.
The five operations only occur when adding a new user and the TTL marker does not exist; returning users require two operations, and new users with an existing marker require three.
The missing TTL is not a permanent leak, as the very next new user will evaluate hasKey(TTL_KEY) as false and consequently re-apply the expiration to the set.
